content.text: How can I define diagram generation logic for an entire project within a visitor pattern?

This method acts as a visitor for a Project node, determining which diagrams are relevant depending on the project's structure and configuration.

```python
def visit_project(self, node):
        """visit a pyreverse.utils.Project node

        create a diagram definition for packages
        """
        mode = self.config.mode
        if len(node.modules) > 1:
            self.pkgdiagram = PackageDiagram("packages %s" % node.name, mode)
        else:
            self.pkgdiagram = None
        self.classdiagram = ClassDiagram("classes %s" % node.name, mode)
```

The code conditionally creates a `PackageDiagram` only if the project contains more than one module to avoid unnecessary artifacts.
What happens if the class definition should also be conditional based on available modules?

If you wanted to hide class diagrams when there are few classes, you could wrap the initialization in an 'if' check similar to how the package diagram is handled. For example:
```python
        if len(node.classes) > 0:
            self.classdiagram = ClassDiagram("classes %s" % node.name, mode)
        else:
            self.classdiagram = None
```
Got it, that makes sense for controlling complexity.
